event: Development of cyst in occipital neck
Cyst started to grow over the past few years
Cyst would periodically drain, scab over and drain again
Portion of the mass fell out 5 days before presentation
Severe diffuse swelling in arms, legs and trunk
Oxygen saturation of 100% on 4-l nasal cannula
Severe upper and lower extremity pitting oedema
Soft to touch with thick oozing purulent drainage
Chest X-ray showed mild interstitial oedema
Transfusion of three units of packed red blood cells
White blood cell count of 24.3*103/μl with 6% band forms
Creatinine doubled from 0.72 mg/dl to 1.46 mg/dL
Disseminated intravascular coagulation secondary to severe sepsis
Bilevel non-invasive positive pressure ventilation started
Pyuria with greater than 180 white blood cells per hpf
Pan-CT scan revealed obstructing left distal ureteric stone
Extraction and stenting of ureteric stone
Unable to be extubated for a day
Chest X-ray showed increasing pulmonary oedema
Oliguric with creatinine elevated to 6.4 mg/dl
Stabilized and transferred back to general medicine service
Pathology showed benign haemangioma with infected haematoma and abscess formation
